name: Asparagus
description: Asparagus is a perennial vegetable known for its tender spears that are a delicacy in various cuisines.
variety_info: Common varieties include Green Marvel and Jersey Knight.

name: Artichoke
description: Artichokes are thistle-like plants cultivated for their edible buds.
variety_info: Popular varieties include Green Globe and Imperial Star.

name: Brussels Sprouts
description: Brussels sprouts are small cabbage-like buds that grow along a tall stalk.
variety_info: Common varieties include Long Island Improved and Jade Cross.
attributes: Rich in vitamins C and K

name: Leek
description: Leeks are a member of the onion family with a mild flavor, often used in soups and stews.
variety_info: Notable varieties include King Richard and American Flag.

name: Shallot
description: Shallots are a type of onion with a milder taste, often used in gourmet cooking.
variety_info: Common varieties include French Gray and Echalion.

name: Scallion
description: Scallions, or green onions, are young onions harvested before they fully mature.
variety_info: Popular varieties include White Lisbon and Evergreen Bunching.
attributes: Rich in vitamins A and C

name: Parsnip
description: Parsnips are root vegetables with a sweet, nutty flavor, often used in soups and roasts.
variety_info: Common varieties include Hollow Crown and White Gem.

name: Rutabaga
description: Rutabagas are root vegetables that are a cross between a cabbage and a turnip.
variety_info: Common varieties include American Purple Top and Laurentian.

name: Kohlrabi
description: Kohlrabi is a cruciferous vegetable with a bulbous stem that can be eaten raw or cooked.
variety_info: Popular varieties include White Vienna and Purple Vienna.

name: Swiss Chard
description: Swiss chard is a leafy green vegetable known for its bright colored stems and nutritious leaves.
variety_info: Common varieties include Rainbow and Fordhook Giant.
attributes: Rich in vitamins A, C, and K

name: Arugula
description: Arugula is a leafy green vegetable known for its peppery flavor and is often used in salads.
variety_info: Common varieties include Wild Arugula and Astro.

name: Collard Greens
description: Collard greens are a type of leafy green vegetable that belong to the cabbage family, known for their thick, dark leaves.
variety_info: Popular varieties include Georgia and Vates.

name: Mustard Greens
description: Mustard greens are leafy vegetables with a peppery flavor, often used in salads and cooking.
variety_info: Varieties include Curly Mustard and Southern Giant.

name: Bok Choy
description: Bok choy is a type of Chinese cabbage with tender green leaves and a mild flavor, popular in stir-fries.
variety_info: Notable varieties include Joi Choi and Mei Qing.

name: Endive
description: Endive is a leafy vegetable known for its slightly bitter taste, often used in salads.
variety_info: Common varieties include Curly Endive and Escarole.

name: Escarole
description: Escarole is a type of endive that has broad, curly leaves and is often used in salads and soups.
variety_info: Varieties include Broadleaf and Batavian.

name: Fennel
description: Fennel is a flavorful herb with a bulbous base and feathery leaves, used in cooking and salads.
variety_info: Varieties include Florence and Common Fennel.

name: Okra (Red)
description: Red okra is a variety of okra that has vibrant red pods and is used in Southern cooking.
variety_info: Notable varieties include Burgundy and Red Velvet.

name: Tomatillo
description: Tomatillos are small, green fruits encased in a papery husk, commonly used in salsas.
variety_info: Common varieties include Verde and Purple.

name: Ground Cherry
description: Ground cherries are small, sweet fruits that grow inside a husk, often used in desserts and jams.
variety_info: Common varieties include Aunt Molly's and Cossack Pine.

name: Rhubarb
description: Rhubarb is a perennial vegetable known for its tart stalks, often used in pies and sauces.
variety_info: Common varieties include Victoria and Crimson Cherry.
attributes: High in fiber, low in calories.

name: Jerusalem Artichoke
description: Also known as sunchoke, this tuber is related to sunflowers and has a nutty flavor.
variety_info: Common varieties include White and Purple.

name: Jicama
description: Jicama is a sweet, crunchy tuber, often eaten raw in salads or as snacks.
variety_info: Commonly grown varieties include Mexican Jicama.
attributes: Low in calories, high in fiber.

name: Cauliflower (Romanesco)
description: Romanesco cauliflower has a unique fractal shape and a slightly nutty flavor.
variety_info: Includes varieties like Veronica and Romanesco.
attributes: Rich in vitamins C and K.

name: Broccoli Rabe
description: A leafy green vegetable with a slightly bitter taste, often used in Italian cuisine.
variety_info: Varieties include Cima di Rapa and Broccoli Raab.
attributes: High in vitamins A and C.

name: Daikon Radish
description: Daikon is a long white radish known for its mild flavor, often used in Asian cuisines.
variety_info: Common varieties include Sakurajima and Minowase.
attributes: Rich in vitamin C and fiber.

name: Horseradish
description: Horseradish is a perennial root vegetable known for its pungent flavor, often used as a condiment.
variety_info: Varieties include Common and Japanese.

name: Serviceberry
description: Serviceberry is a deciduous tree known for its white flowers and edible berries.
variety_info: Includes varieties like Saskatoon and Autumn Brilliance.

name: Huckleberry
description: Huckleberries are small, sweet berries that grow on shrubs, often found in mountainous regions.
variety_info: Includes varieties like Blue and Black Huckleberry.

name: Salmonberry
description: Salmonberry is a deciduous shrub known for its bright orange to red berries.
variety_info: Includes varieties like Rubus spectabilis.
attributes: Rich in vitamin C, edible fruit.

name: Thimbleberry
description: Thimbleberry is a deciduous shrub known for its sweet, red berries.
variety_info: Common variety includes Rubus parviflorus.

name: Boysenberry
description: Boysenberry is a hybrid berry known for its sweet-tart flavor.
variety_info: A cross between raspberry, blackberry, and loganberry.

name: Cloudberry
description: Cloudberry is a unique fruit-bearing plant found in northern regions.
variety_info: Rubus chamaemorus is the primary species.

name: Lingonberry
description: Lingonberry is a small red berry with a tart flavor, popular in Nordic countries.
variety_info: Vaccinium vitis-idaea is the common species.

name: Currant (Red)
description: Red currants are small, tart berries often used in jams and jellies.
variety_info: Ribes rubrum is the common species.

name: Currant (Black)
description: Black currants are known for their rich flavor and medicinal properties.
variety_info: Ribes nigrum is the common species.

name: Chokecherry
description: Chokecherry is a small tree with clusters of white flowers and dark cherries.
variety_info: Prunus virginiana is the common species.

name: Black Cherry
description: Black Cherry is a large tree known for its dark fruit and beautiful foliage.
variety_info: Prunus serotina is the common species.

name: American Plum
description: American Plum is a small to medium-sized tree with sweet, edible plums.
variety_info: Prunus americana is the common species.

name: Wild Strawberry
description: Wild Strawberry is a low-growing plant that produces small, sweet strawberries.
variety_info: Fragaria vesca is the common species.

name: Beach Plum
description: Beach Plum is a small shrub producing edible plums, typically found in coastal areas.
variety_info: Common varieties include 'Native Beach Plum' and 'Plymouth Beach Plum'.

name: Mayhaw
description: Mayhaw is a small tree known for its sweet, tart fruit often used in jellies.
variety_info: Popular varieties include 'Burgundy', 'Hawthorn', and 'Red Star'.

name: Quince
description: Quince is a fruit-bearing tree valued for its aromatic fruit, often used in cooking.
variety_info: Notable varieties include 'Pineapple Quince' and ' Smyrna'.

name: Fig
description: Fig trees produce sweet fruits that are enjoyed fresh or dried.
variety_info: Varieties include 'Brown Turkey', 'Celeste', and 'Kadota'.

name: Pomegranate
description: Pomegranate trees produce large, round fruits filled with juicy seeds.
variety_info: Common varieties include 'Wonderful' and 'Angel Red'.

name: Kiwi (Hardy)
description: Hardy kiwi vines produce small, edible fruits that are sweet and nutritious.
variety_info: Popular varieties include 'Hardy Arctic' and 'Baby Kiwi'.

name: Honeyberry
description: Honeyberry is a shrub that produces sweet, blue berries similar to blueberries.
variety_info: Varieties include 'Blue Moon' and 'Honeybee'.

name: Aronia (Chokeberry)
description: Aronia, or chokeberry, produces small, dark berries that are high in antioxidants.
variety_info: Common varieties include 'Viking' and 'Nero'.

name: Pecan
description: Pecan trees produce large, flavorful nuts and are a popular choice in southern gardens.
variety_info: Common varieties include 'Pawnee' and 'Desirable'.

name: American Hazelnut
description: American Hazelnut is a shrub that produces edible nuts and provides excellent wildlife habitat.
variety_info: Common varieties include 'Tonda di Giffoni' and 'Barcelona'.

name: Butternut
description: A sweet, nutty squash with a tan skin and orange flesh.
variety_info: Commonly grown for its sweet flavor and nutritious flesh.

name: Chestnut
description: A deciduous tree known for its delicious nuts.
variety_info: American and European varieties are common.

name: English Walnut
description: A large tree known for its edible nuts.
variety_info: Commonly cultivated for its high-quality nuts.

name: Chamomile
description: A flowering plant known for its calming herbal properties.
variety_info: German and Roman varieties are popular.

name: Lemongrass
description: A tropical plant known for its citrus flavor.
variety_info: Commonly used in culinary dishes.

name: Bay Laurel
description: An aromatic evergreen shrub used for culinary purposes.
variety_info: Commonly used in cooking and as a decorative plant.

name: Lovage
description: A perennial herb with a flavor similar to celery.
variety_info: Used in soups and salads for its flavor.

name: Sorrel
description: A leafy herb with a tart flavor, often used in salads.
variety_info: Common varieties include French and garden sorrel.

name: Lemon Balm
description: A fragrant herb known for its lemony scent and flavor.
variety_info: Commonly used in teas and culinary dishes.

name: Fennel (Herb)
description: An aromatic herb with a sweet, anise-like flavor.
variety_info: Commonly used in cooking and herbal remedies.

name: Anise Hyssop
description: Anise Hyssop is a perennial herb with fragrant leaves that have a sweet, anise-like flavor. It attracts pollinators and can be used in teas and culinary dishes.
variety_info: Common variety includes 'Golden Jubilee' which has golden-green leaves.

name: Bee Balm
description: Bee Balm is a vibrant perennial flower known for attracting bees, butterflies, and hummingbirds. It has aromatic leaves and comes in various colors.
variety_info: Popular varieties include 'Jacob Cline' and 'Marshall's Delight'.

name: Stevia
description: Stevia is a sweet herb used as a natural sweetener. It is easy to grow and has a unique flavor.
variety_info: Common variety is 'Stevia rebaudiana'.

name: Horsemint
description: Horsemint is a wildflower known for its unique fragrance and attractive flowers, often used in herbal teas.
variety_info: Common variety is 'Monarda punctata'.

name: Joe-Pye Weed
description: Joe-Pye Weed is a tall perennial that produces large clusters of pink flowers, attracting a variety of pollinators.
variety_info: Varieties include 'Eupatorium purpureum' and 'Eupatorium maculatum'.

name: Blazing Star (Liatris)
description: Blazing Star is a striking perennial flower known for its tall spikes of purple flowers and ability to attract butterflies.
variety_info: Common varieties include 'Liatris spicata'.

name: Cardinal Flower
description: Cardinal Flower is a perennial known for its striking red flowers that attract hummingbirds and butterflies.
variety_info: Varieties include 'Lobelia cardinalis'.

name: Great Blue Lobelia
description: Great Blue Lobelia is a perennial flower with blue spikes that are highly attractive to pollinators, especially bees.
variety_info: Common variety is 'Lobelia siphilitica'.

name: Butterfly Weed
description: Butterfly Weed is a vibrant orange flower that attracts butterflies and is a critical nectar source for pollinators.
variety_info: Common variety is 'Asclepias tuberosa'.

name: Golden Alexander
description: Golden Alexander is a perennial wildflower with yellow flowers that bloom in early spring, attracting various insects.
variety_info: Common variety is 'Zizia aurea'.

name: Coreopsis
description: Coreopsis, commonly known as tickseed, is a hardy perennial flower that thrives in sunny gardens.
variety_info: Includes varieties like 'Moonbeam' and 'Early Sunrise'.

name: Blanketflower
description: Blanketflower is a vibrant perennial known for its daisy-like flowers that bloom throughout the summer.
variety_info: Popular varieties include 'Gaillardia aristata' and 'Goblin'.

name: Penstemon
description: Penstemon, or beardtongue, features tubular flowers and is a favorite among hummingbirds.
variety_info: Varieties include 'Husker Red' and 'Margarita BOP'.

name: New England Aster
description: New England Aster is a late-blooming perennial that adds vibrant color to gardens in the fall.
variety_info: Includes varieties such as 'Purple Dome' and 'Alma Potschke'.

name: Wild Geranium
description: Wild Geranium is a native perennial that blooms in spring with lovely purple flowers.
variety_info: Common variety is 'Geranium maculatum'.

name: Solomon's Seal
description: Solomon's Seal is a shade-loving perennial with arching stems and bell-shaped flowers.
variety_info: Common variety is 'Polygonatum biflorum'.
treatment_methods: Good air circulation, remove dead leaves

name: Mayapple
description: Mayapple is a unique perennial that produces umbrella-like leaves and white flowers in spring.
variety_info: Scientific name is 'Podophyllum peltatum'.

name: Bloodroot
description: Bloodroot is a spring ephemeral that blooms with striking white flowers before the leaves fully unfurl.
variety_info: Scientific name is 'Sanguinaria canadensis'.

name: Jack-in-the-Pulpit
description: Jack-in-the-Pulpit is a unique woodland plant known for its distinctive hooded flowers.
variety_info: Scientific name is 'Arisaema triphyllum'.
treatment_methods: Good air circulation, remove affected leaves

name: Dutchman's Breeches
description: Dutchman's Breeches is a charming spring wildflower with unique, heart-shaped flowers.
variety_info: Scientific name is 'Dicentra cucullaria'.

name: Shooting Star
description: A delicate wildflower with unique, star-shaped blooms that attract pollinators.
variety_info: Various colors including white, pink, and purple.

name: Prairie Smoke
description: A unique wildflower known for its feathery seed heads resembling smoke.
variety_info: Typically has pink to purple flowers.

name: Evening Primrose
description: Known for its bright yellow flowers that bloom in the evening.
variety_info: Common varieties include Oenothera biennis.

name: Wild Columbine (Red)
description: A native wildflower with striking red and yellow flowers that attract hummingbirds.
variety_info: Aquilegia canadensis is the common variety.

name: American Beech
description: A large deciduous tree known for its smooth gray bark and dense canopy.
variety_info: Fagus grandifolia is the common variety.

name: American Elm
description: A tall tree with a broad canopy, known for its resistance to urban pollution.
variety_info: Ulmus americana is the common variety.

name: Tulip Poplar
description: A tall tree known for its unique tulip-shaped flowers and yellow-green foliage.
variety_info: Liriodendron tulipifera is the common variety.

name: American Basswood
description: A medium to large tree known for its heart-shaped leaves and fragrant flowers.
variety_info: Tilia americana is the common variety.

name: Bald Cypress
description: A unique tree that thrives in wet conditions, known for its distinctive knees.
variety_info: Taxodium distichum is the common variety.

name: Sweetgum
description: A striking tree known for its star-shaped leaves and spiky fruit.
variety_info: Liquidambar styraciflua is the common variety.

name: Black Tupelo (Blackgum)
description: A medium to large deciduous tree known for its vibrant fall foliage and adaptability to different soil types.
variety_info: Varieties include 'Wildfire' and 'Green Mountain'.

name: Sassafras
description: A deciduous tree known for its aromatic leaves and distinctive lobed foliage.
variety_info: Common varieties include 'Sassafras albidum'.

name: Eastern Red Cedar
description: A hardy evergreen tree with scale-like leaves and aromatic wood, commonly used for fencing and furniture.
variety_info: Includes varieties such as 'Canaertii' and 'Emerald'.

name: Tamarack (Eastern Larch)
description: A deciduous conifer that loses its needles in the fall, known for its beautiful golden-yellow autumn color.
variety_info: Includes varieties such as 'Larix laricina'.

name: White Ash
description: A large deciduous tree valued for its strong wood and attractive foliage, which turns bright yellow in autumn.
variety_info: Varieties include 'Fraxinus americana'.

name: Green Ash
description: A versatile and hardy tree often used for urban landscaping, known for its compound leaves and adaptability.
variety_info: Varieties include 'Fraxinus pennsylvanica'.

name: Paper Birch
description: A striking deciduous tree with white bark and delicate leaves, often found in northern climates.
variety_info: Includes varieties such as 'Betula papyrifera'.

name: American Holly
description: An evergreen shrub or small tree with glossy leaves and red berries, often used in holiday decorations.
variety_info: Includes varieties such as 'Ilex opaca'.

name: Osage Orange
description: A small to medium-sized tree with distinctive yellow-green fruit, often used for hedges and windbreaks.
variety_info: Includes varieties such as 'Maclura pomifera'.

name: Black Locust
description: A fast-growing tree known for its fragrant flowers and ability to thrive in poor soils.
variety_info: Includes varieties such as 'Robinia pseudoacacia'.

name: Honey Locust
description: A fast-growing tree with a wide canopy, known for its small leaflets and fragrant flowers.
variety_info: Varieties include Thornless Honey Locust and Sunburst.

name: Kentucky Coffeetree
description: A large deciduous tree that produces unique seed pods and has a rugged appearance.
variety_info: Notable varieties include 'Soil Saver' and 'Moraine'.

name: American Hornbeam
description: A small to medium-sized deciduous tree known for its smooth gray bark and dense foliage.
variety_info: Common varieties include 'Fastigiata' and 'Riversii'.

name: Witch Hazel
description: An ornamental shrub known for its fragrant yellow flowers that bloom in late winter.
variety_info: Popular varieties include 'Hamamelis virginiana' and 'Hamamelis x intermedia'.

name: Spicebush
description: A fragrant shrub that produces small, bright yellow flowers in early spring.
variety_info: Varieties include 'Lindera benzoin'.

name: Buttonbush
description: A wetland shrub known for its spherical flower clusters and ability to attract pollinators.
variety_info: Notable variety includes 'Cephalanthus occidentalis'.

name: Ninebark
description: A hardy shrub with attractive peeling bark and clusters of white to pink flowers.
variety_info: Common varieties include 'Diabolo' and 'Summer Wine'.

name: New Jersey Tea
description: A low-growing shrub known for its white flower clusters and ability to attract butterflies.
variety_info: Variety includes 'Ceanothus americanus'.

name: Inkberry
description: An evergreen shrub known for its glossy leaves and dark purple berries.
variety_info: Varieties include 'Ilex glabra'.

name: American Beautyberry
description: A deciduous shrub known for its vibrant purple berries that attract wildlife.
variety_info: Varieties include 'Callicarpa americana'.

name: Arrowwood Viburnum
description: A hardy shrub known for its beautiful white flowers and vibrant fall foliage.
variety_info: Includes various cultivars with different growth habits.

name: Wax Myrtle
description: An evergreen shrub that is aromatic and attracts birds with its berries.
variety_info: Various cultivars available with differing heights.

name: Red Osier Dogwood
description: A deciduous shrub known for its striking red stems and white flowers.
variety_info: Multiple varieties with varied stem colors.

name: Winterberry Holly
description: A deciduous holly that produces bright red berries in winter, attracting birds.

name: Highbush Cranberry (Viburnum)
description: A shrub with clusters of white flowers followed by red berries, often used in jams.
variety_info: Various cultivars with differing berry flavors.

name: Virginia Creeper
description: A vigorous vine known for its attractive foliage and bright red fall color.
variety_info: Multiple varieties with varying growth habits.

name: Trumpet Vine
description: A vigorous vine with large trumpet-shaped flowers, attracting hummingbirds.
variety_info: Different cultivars with varying flower colors.

name: Maypop (Passionflower)
description: A climbing vine with unique flowers and edible fruit.
variety_info: Different varieties with varying fruit flavors.

name: American Wisteria
description: A vigorous vine with cascading clusters of fragrant purple flowers.
variety_info: Different cultivars with varying flower colors.

name: Prairie Dropseed
description: A native grass that forms dense clumps and produces fine, fragrant seeds.
variety_info: Various cultivars available for different landscapes.

name: Indiangrass
description: Indiangrass is a perennial grass known for its tall stature and golden seed heads. It provides excellent wildlife habitat.
variety_info: There are several varieties of Indiangrass suited for different soil types and climates.

name: Sideoats Grama
description: Sideoats Grama is a warm-season grass that is characterized by its unique seed heads that grow on one side of the stem.
variety_info: Common varieties include 'Cody' and 'Largo'.

name: Eastern Gama Grass
description: Eastern Gama Grass is a perennial grass that grows in clumps and is valued for its forage quality.
variety_info: Varieties include 'Gama' and 'Florida'.

name: Canada Wildrye
description: Canada Wildrye is a perennial grass that is often used for erosion control and wildlife habitat.
variety_info: Varieties include 'Cree' and 'Pawnee'.

name: Bearberry (Kinnikinnick)
description: Bearberry is a low-growing shrub that produces red berries and is commonly found in dry, sandy soils.
variety_info: Common varieties include 'Vancouver' and 'Kinnickinnick'.

name: Partridgeberry
description: Partridgeberry is a creeping herb known for its red berries and is often used in ornamental gardens.
variety_info: Typically grown from seed or cuttings.

name: Wintergreen
description: Wintergreen is a low-growing herb known for its aromatic leaves and red berries.
variety_info: Commonly grown from seeds or division.

name: Chicken of the Woods
description: Chicken of the Woods is a large edible mushroom known for its bright colors and meaty texture.
variety_info: Commonly found on oak and other hardwoods.

name: Maitake
description: Maitake is a popular edible mushroom known for its unique frilled appearance and rich flavor.
variety_info: Grows at the base of oak trees.

name: Reishi
description: Reishi is a medicinal mushroom known for its potential health benefits and distinctive appearance.
variety_info: Commonly found on decaying wood.

name: Turkey Tail
description: Turkey Tail is a polypore mushroom known for its colorful, fan-shaped caps and medicinal properties.
variety_info: Commonly found in various colors including brown, orange, and blue.

name: Shiitake
description: Shiitake mushrooms are renowned for their rich flavor and are widely cultivated for culinary use.
variety_info: Includes varieties such as Black Diamond and Golden.
